organisations_reference_number.definition: A code identifying an Organisation associated with an object.
organisations_reference_number.how_to_record: Record a unique number or code uniquely identifying the Organisation where an Organisation’s MDA code does not exist. Maintain a list of standard codes used.
organisations_reference_number.use: Record once only for an Organisation.

organisations_address.definition: The address where an Organisation can be contacted.
organisations_address.how_to_record: It will be necessary to record several units of information, including for example, a building name, street name and town/city. The descriptions for these information units are gathered together under the Address heading.
organisations_address.use: As many times as required for an Organisation.

organisations_additions_to_name.definition: Additional information about the identity of an Organisation.
organisations_additions_to_name.how_to_record: This information is used to uniquely identify an Organisation in the event of duplicate names. Record any information which serves to differentiate between this Organisation and another with a similar name.
examples: Oxford [In the case of the Museum of Modern Art]
organisations_additions_to_name.use: Record as many times as required for an Organisation.

organisations_contact_name.definition: The representative of an Organisation who is normally liaised with.
organisations_contact_name.how_to_record: It will be necessary to use several units of information, including, for example, a forename and surname. The descriptions for these information units are gathered together under the Person heading.
organisations_contact_name.use: As many times as required for an Organisation.

organisations_foundation_date.definition: The date on which an Organisation formally came into being.
organisations_foundation_date.how_to_record: It may be necessary to use more than one unit of information to describe this date, eg a range of dates or a textual expression of the date. Descriptions of these units of information are gathered together under the Date heading.
organisations_foundation_date.use: Record once only for an Organisation.

organisations_foundation_place.definition: Information about the place where an Organisation came into being.
organisations_foundation_place.how_to_record: It may be necessary to use several units of information, including, for example, village or a country. The descriptions for these information units are gathered together under the Place heading.
organisations_foundation_place.use: Record once only for an Organisation.

organisations_dissolution_date.definition: The date on which an Organisation formally ceased to be. This may be the date on which it was taken over or wound up.
organisations_dissolution_date.how_to_record: It may be necessary to use more than one unit of information to describe this date, e.g. a range of dates or a textual expression of the date. Descriptions of these units of information are gathered together under the Date heading.
organisations_dissolution_date.use: Record once only for an Organisation.

organisations_function.definition: The activities which make up the main business of an Organisation.
organisations_function.how_to_record: Use a single term. Maintain a list of standard terms, based on a recognised terminology source.
organisations_function.use: As many times as required for an Organisation.

organisations_group.definition: The social, socio professional, ethnic, or culture group to which the Organisation belongs.
organisations_group.how_to_record: Use a single term. Maintain a list of standard terms, based on a recognised terminology source.
organisations_group.use: As many times as required for an Organisation. Note that some terms in common archaeological use can have a range of connotations and could be recorded under different headings, eg ‘6ic’ might be recorded as Date – period (chronological), Object production people (ethnic) or Associated cultural affinity (having intrinsic features and/or contextual associations which link object with “Belgic” material culture)

organisations_history.definition: Information about the history of the Organisation.
organisations_history.how_to_record: Use normal grammar and punctuation. Describe the Organisation, or its work in general. Do not record information which is specific to an object; use instead Production note or Object history note.
examples: Underwent several changes of mission in the 1930s before establishing its current role in the market.
organisations_history.use: Record once only for an Organisation.

organisations_mda_code.definition: The code uniquely identifying an Organisation. The MDA Code system is managed by Collections Trust in the UK.
organisations_mda_code.how_to_record: Usually five capital letters. Use especially to identify objects coming from outside the organisation.
organisations_mda_code.use: Record once only for an Organisation.

organisations_main_body.how_to_record: Use the name exactly as given by the Organisation. Maintain a list of names of organisations.
organisations_main_body.use: Record once only for an Organisation.

organisations_subbody.definition: The name of the sub-body of an Organisation.
organisations_subbody.how_to_record: As many times as required. Sub-bodies usually have generic names implying they are part of a larger unit and cannot be recorded as a main body. Maintain a list of names of sub-bodies for each Organisation.
organisations_subbody.use: As many times as required for an Organisation.

organisations_association.definition: The way in which an Organisation is associated with an object.
organisations_association.how_to_record: Use a single term, without punctuation or capitalisation. Maintain a list of standard terms.
organisations_association.use: As many times as required for an object.
